openingMessage: If you can't communicate, you can't lead. Communicate everything.
openingWhy: The day crew knew the line still had pressure on it. The night crew didn't. Everything that lives between those two sentences is a hospital visit. Say it early, say it plain, write it in the handover — information that doesn't move is how good crews hurt each other.
leadershipThought: The most dangerous driver on the road is the one who has driven that route fifty times. Familiarity turns your eyes off. Awareness means driving mile 50 like it's mile 1 — because the deer, the hotshot truck, and the washout don't care how well you know the road.

Exclusion Zone Breach — Third-Party Personnel (Near Miss) (Line of Fire)
When SimOps create unplanned traffic near your exclusion zone, proactive interception by all crew members — not just the safety tech — is what prevents a line-of-fire exposure.

todaysChallenge: The best hand on location can't watch every line. Today, make one deliberate hand-off: tell the next person exactly what you checked and what you're not sure of.
todaysChallengeSubtext: “Talent wins games, but teamwork and intelligence win championships.” — Michael Jordan
